title: Grammar · English C2
scope: C2 (Cambridge Proficiency) is about mastery of nuance rather than new structures: advanced inversion and fronting, nuanced modality and hedging, idiom and collocation, complex impersonal and passive structures, advanced cohesion, register shifts and subtle concessive/conditional forms. Boundary: the ceiling of the language, aiming at near-native precision and style.

blocks.text: Fronting and inversion are used for strong emphasis and a formal, literary register.

rows: So great was the demand that they sold out.
Such was his anger that he left.
Little did they know what awaited them.
No sooner had we sat down than the phone rang.

blocks.text: Cautious, indirect language to soften claims, typical of academic and diplomatic register.
It would appear that … · This may suggest … · One could argue that … · There is a tendency to …
Natural word partnerships and idiomatic phrases that mark proficiency; their meaning is not literal.

rows: Bear in mind that prices vary.
The result was a foregone conclusion.
The stress took its toll on her.

blocks.text: Impersonal and passive forms distance the writer and add formality.
It is widely held that … · It cannot be denied that … · Steps are being taken to address the issue.
Sophisticated reference, ellipsis and substitution keep long texts cohesive and concise.
Some support the plan; others, less so. The former option is cheaper; the latter, faster.

rows: Be that as it may, we must proceed.
in case / so as not to
He spoke quietly, lest he be overheard.
Should you need help, call me.
